## Terms of Service

**Last Updated:** 22 August 2025

### 1. Introduction and Acceptance of Terms

1.1. These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the website [https://www.tahi.studio](https://www.tahi.studio) (the "Website") and the services provided by Tahi Studio Limited, a company registered in New Zealand (NZBN: 9429052709685) ("Tahi Studio", "we", "us", "our").

1.2. By accessing our Website or purchasing our Services, you ("Client", "you") agree to be bound by these Terms. If you are using the Services on behalf of an organisation, you are agreeing to these Terms for that organisation and warrant that you have the authority to bind that organisation to these Terms.

1.3. We reserve the right to amend these Terms from time to time. We will provide notice of any material changes by posting a notification on your client dashboard or by sending an email to your registered address. Your continued use of the Services after such changes will constitute your acceptance of the new Terms.

### 2. The Services

2.1. Tahi Studio provides branding, web design, Webflow development, and SEO services through a variety of packages, including recurring subscriptions ("Subscriptions"), hourly packages, and fixed-price one-off projects ("Projects").

2.2. **Subscription Services**:

a. Subscriptions provide you with access to our services for a recurring fee. You may submit unlimited service requests ("Requests") through your dedicated client dashboard.

b. Each Request is addressed one at a time. Work on a new Request will typically commence within one to three business days after the preceding Request is formally marked as complete. This timeframe is an estimate and not a guarantee.

c. You may pause your Subscription at any time through your client dashboard. The remaining time on your Subscription will be credited to your account and will not expire. We will not perform any work on your Requests while your Subscription is paused.

d. You may cancel your Subscription at any time. Cancellation will take effect at the end of the current billing cycle, and you will not be charged again.

2.3. **One-Off Projects**:

a. For standalone Projects, we will provide a detailed scope of work ("Scope of Work"), timeline, and fixed price for your approval. This Scope of Work may be provided via email or as a separate document. Once agreed upon, the Scope of Work will form part of this agreement.

b. We offer a two (2) week bug-fix period for Projects, starting from the official launch date. During this period, we will fix any bugs or errors arising directly from our work at no additional cost. This warranty does not cover issues caused by third-party plugins, hosting, or changes made by you or any other party.

c. Billing for Projects is structured with fifty percent (50%) of the total project fee due upfront before work commences. The remaining fifty percent (50%) is due upon completion of the work, prior to the final delivery of files or website launch.

### 3. Client Obligations

3.1. To ensure the timely and successful delivery of the Services, you agree to provide all necessary information, materials, and content ("Client Content") in a timely manner.

3.2. You are responsible for providing prompt and clear feedback on all deliverables. Any delay in providing feedback or Client Content may result in a corresponding delay in project timelines. While your Subscription remains active during such delays, our obligation to meet estimated timelines will be adjusted accordingly.

3.3. You warrant that you have all necessary rights and permissions to provide the Client Content to us and that its use will not infringe upon the intellectual property rights of any third party.

### 4. Fees, Payment, and Refunds

4.1. Payments for Services can be made via our third-party payment provider, Stripe, or through direct bank transfer.

4.2. **Subscription Fees:** Subscription fees are billed on a recurring basis (e.g., monthly, quarterly, or yearly) as per the selected plan. By purchasing a Subscription, you authorise us to charge your payment method for the recurring fees.

4.3. **Project Fees:** For Projects, an initial non-refundable deposit of fifty percent (50%) of the total fee is required to secure the project booking and commence work. The final fifty percent (50%) is payable upon completion, before the transfer of final deliverables.

4.4. All fees are exclusive of any applicable taxes, which you are responsible for paying.

4.5. All fees are non-refundable except where required by New Zealand consumer law, or at our sole discretion in exceptional circumstances.

### 5. Intellectual Property Rights

5.1. **Client Ownership:** Upon our receipt of your full payment for the relevant Services, you will own the unique, final deliverables created specifically for you as part of the Services. We will not use these specific final deliverables for any other client.

5.2. **Our Retained Rights:**

a. Tahi Studio retains ownership of all pre-existing materials, know-how, code, templates, processes, and other intellectual property used to provide the Services ("Tahi IP"). We grant you a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use any Tahi IP that is incorporated into your final deliverables.

b. Unless you explicitly opt-out in writing, you grant Tahi Studio a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use your business name, logo, and any public-facing deliverables (e.g., your completed website) in our portfolio, case studies, and other marketing materials. We will not disclose any of your confidential information without your prior written consent.

### 6. Confidentiality

6.1. Both parties agree to keep all non-public information received from the other party confidential and not to disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except where required by law.

### 7. Subcontractors

7.1. We may engage vetted subcontractors to perform some of the Services on our behalf. We remain fully responsible for the performance of the Services and the compliance of our subcontractors with these Terms.

### 8. Limitation of Liability and Disclaimer

8.1. To the maximum extent permitted by law, the total liability of Tahi Studio for any claim arising out of or in connection with the Services or these Terms, whether in contract, tort, or otherwise, shall be limited to the total fees paid by you for the specific Service in the one (1) month preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

8.2. We are not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to, loss of profits, data, or goodwill.

8.3. The Services are provided "as is" without any warranties, express or implied, except for the bug-fix warranty for Projects as described in Clause 2.3 (b). We strive for high-quality work, but we do not guarantee that the Services will be error-free or uninterrupted.

### 9. General Provisions

9.1. **Governing Law and Dispute Resolution:** These Terms shall be governed by the laws of New Zealand. In the event of a dispute, the parties agree to first attempt to resolve it through good-faith negotiation. If negotiation fails, the parties agree to enter into mandatory mediation before commencing any court proceedings. Any legal action shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of New Zealand.

9.2. **Age Requirement:** You must be at least 18 years of age to use our Services.

9.3. **Prohibited Uses:** You agree not to use our Website or Services for any unlawful purpose or to engage in any activity that could harm, disable, or impair the Website.

9.4. **Contact:** For any questions regarding these Terms, please contact us at business@tahi.studio.

---

## Privacy Policy

**Last Updated:** 22 August 2025

### 1. Introduction

Tahi Studio Limited ("Tahi Studio", "we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website [https://www.tahi.studio](https://www.tahi.studio), use our client dashboard, or engage with our services. We process all personal data in compliance with New Zealand's Privacy Act 2020.

### 2. Information We Collect

We may collect the following types of personal data:

- **Identity Data:** Your first name, last name, and company name.
- **Contact Data:** Your email address and billing address.
- **Financial Data:** While we do not store your full payment card details, our payment processors (Stripe or direct bank transfer) do. We may have access to billing address and bank account information to process payments.
- **Technical Data:** Your Internet Protocol (IP) address, browser type and version, time zone setting and location, and other technology on the devices you use to access our Website.
- **Usage Data:** Information about how you use our Website and Services, collected via analytics tools.

### 3. How We Collect Your Data

We collect data through:

- **Direct Interactions:** When you fill in forms on our Website, purchase a service, or communicate with us via email.
- **Automated Technologies:** As you interact with our Website, we automatically collect Technical and Usage Data through cookies and similar technologies. Our cookie banner provides notice of this collection.
- **Client Dashboard Usage:** When you use our client dashboard, we collect Usage and Technical data to monitor performance, track conversions, identify technical issues, and improve our services.

### 4. How We Use Your Personal Data

We use your personal data for the following purposes:

- To provide and manage your access to our Services, including your client dashboard.
- To process payments and manage your account.
- To communicate with you about your projects and our services.
- To improve our Website, services, and marketing.
- For marketing communications, which you can opt-out of at any time.

### 5. Disclosure of Your Personal Data

We do not sell your personal data. We may share your data with the following categories of trusted third-party service providers who help us operate our business:

- **Client Portal & Project Management:** ManyRequests (client dashboard), Google Workspace (email, file storage, documents), Figma (design collaboration), and Loom (video communication).
- **Payment Processing:** Stripe (for credit card payments) and our banking partners (for direct bank transfers).
- **Marketing & CRM:** MailerLite (for email marketing).
- **Analytics & Advertising:** Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Google Ads, and Matomo (to understand website traffic and ad performance).
- **Affiliate Program Management:** Rewardful (to manage our affiliate program).
- **Process Automation:** Zapier (to connect and automate our software tools).
- **Subcontractors:** We may share necessary project information with vetted subcontractors who are bound by confidentiality obligations.
- **Legal Authorities:** If required by law or to protect our legal rights.

### 6. International Data Transfers

Many of our third-party service providers are based outside of New Zealand, which means your data may be transferred internationally. We take steps to ensure that your data is treated securely and in accordance with this Privacy Policy, even when it is transferred to other countries.

### 7. Data Security

We have implemented appropriate security measures to prevent your personal data from being accidentally lost, used, or accessed in an unauthorised way. These measures include two-factor authentication and limiting access to your personal data to those employees and third parties who have a business need to know.

### 8. Data Retention

We will only retain your personal data for as long as is necessary to fulfil the purposes we collected it for. To comply with our legal, accounting, and reporting requirements, we will retain essential client and project data for a period of seven (7) years after the completion of services or termination of your account. You may request the deletion of your data at any time, subject to our legal obligations.

### 9. Your Legal Rights

Under New Zealand's Privacy Act 2020, you have rights including:

- **The right to access:** You can ask for a copy of your personal data.
- **The right to rectification:** You can ask us to correct any inaccurate information.

You can access and update some of your information directly through your client dashboard. To exercise any of your other rights, please contact us.

### 10. Children's Privacy

Our services are not intended for and we do not knowingly collect data from individuals under the age of 18.

### 11. Contact Us

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise any of your legal rights, please contact us at: business@tahi.studio

**Schema Markup Plan for Tahi Studio**
======================================

This document outlines the complete schema markup strategy for the Tahi
Studio website, designed for implementation with the **Schemaflow** app.

The plan is structured around adding individual schema blocks (e.g.,
Organization, WebPage, FAQPage) for each page or template. Each code
block below represents a separate schema item that should be created and
applied within Schemaflow.

**Global Schema (Applied to All Pages)**

This is the foundational layer applied to every page on the site to
establish a consistent brand entity.

pages.title: Growth Priorities 2026
pages.content:
# Growth Priorities 2026
*Living document. Update status and notes as things move.*
*Created March 2026*

---

## What This Is

A ranked list of the highest-ROI moves available to Tahi right now. Not everything on here is urgent, but everything on here will either make money, save significant time, or compound into something that does both. The ranking is based on effort-to-return ratio, automation potential, and strategic fit.

Each item has: what it is, why it matters, what it needs to happen, and current status.

---

## Ranking Key

**Tier 1: Do these first.** High ROI, relatively low effort, foundational for everything else.
**Tier 2: Do these next.** Strong ROI, slightly more build time, or depend on Tier 1 being in place.
**Tier 3: Do when ready.** High ceiling, more complex, or timed to a product launch.

---

## Tier 1

### 1. Email welcome sequence for Project Calculator subscribers

**What it is:** A four-email automated sequence that goes to every new subscriber who downloads the Project Calculator. Runs automatically forever once built.

**Why it matters:** The Figma template delivery email gets 73% open rate and 52% click rate. That is peak engagement, and it currently leads nowhere. A welcome sequence turns a one-time freebie transaction into an ongoing relationship. This is the single highest-leverage automation available because it compounds: every new subscriber benefits from it, forever, with no additional effort. It is also the foundation for the affiliate pipeline (see item 5).

**Actual structure (as built):**
- Email 1 (day 2): "You're Probably Undercharging" — Liam intro, why he built the calculator, sets up the series with a reader-centric hook on pricing confidence
- Email 2 (day 5): "What to Say When a Client Says It's Too Expensive" — how to handle price pushback without discounting, value-anchoring tactics, how Tahi gets ahead of objections
- Email 3 (day 10): "Why Retainers Beat Projects Every Time" — making the case for retainer income over project cycles, how to pitch it at project end
- Email 4 (day 16): "On Working Together" — affiliate/referral programme intro, bidirectional nature of the relationship, invitation to call or connect on LinkedIn

**Automation details:** MailerLite automation ID 181531342084768840. Built and live. Trigger updated to the Project Calculator group. See 11-tools-and-tech-stack.md or MailerLite MCP for full step structure.

**Automation potential:** 10/10. Once live, it runs without intervention.

**Status:** Live as of March 2026.

---

### 2. Monthly newsletter to the F&A audience

**What it is:** A regular email to the freelancer and agency owner segment of the list. Monthly cadence. Format: a short personal note from Liam covering one thing he noticed or learned this month, plus one or two value-add items (a tip, a resource, a link worth reading). Not a blog digest. Something written for the inbox.

**Why it matters:** The list has 3,695 active subscribers and they have heard almost nothing since they signed up. A monthly newsletter is the minimum viable relationship. It keeps the list warm, builds trust over time, and creates a ready audience for every template launch, Nodeo announcement, or affiliate push. Without this, every future campaign sends to a cold list.

**What it needs:** A consistent writing habit. Monthly is achievable. The format should be locked in and templated so it takes under two hours to write each month. Aim for under 400 words per send.

**Automation potential:** Low on the writing side, but the send process can be templated and systematic.

**Status:** Not started.

---

### 3. HubSpot CRM setup and sales pipeline tracking

**What it is:** A properly configured HubSpot pipeline that tracks every lead from first contact through to closed deal. Deal stages, contact enrichment, activity logging, and a clear view of pipeline value at any time.

**Why it matters:** Tahi cannot reliably forecast revenue, spot bottlenecks in the sales process, or know where leads are going cold without this. It is also the foundation for any future client nurture sequence. ManyRequests handles the delivery side. HubSpot handles the pre-client commercial relationship.

**What it needs:** Pipeline stages defined, contact import or sync from existing sources, basic automation for stage transitions. See 09-sales-strategy.md for the sales process that this should mirror.

**Automation potential:** High once set up. Lead source tracking, deal value reporting, and follow-up reminders all become automatic.

**Status:** Not started.

---

### 4. Code snippets and free resource library

**What it is:** A regularly updated library of code snippets, tutorials, and free resources for Webflow designers and developers. Published on the Tahi site. Distributed via email, LinkedIn, and potentially as gated downloads that grow the list further.

**Why it matters:** Liam already has a lot of this content in his head and in drafts. It is not published. This audience (freelancers, designers, developers) actively searches for exactly this kind of content. Publishing it consistently builds SEO, builds the personal brand, fills the content calendar without having to manufacture topics, and gives the email list something worth opening. A new code snippet every two to three weeks is a manageable cadence.

**What it needs:** A consistent format for how snippets are packaged and published. A backlog to draw from (Liam has said he has many ideas not yet written up). A simple system to go from idea to published.

**Automation potential:** Medium. The publishing workflow can be systematised. Distribution via Zapier to LinkedIn is already partly in place.

**Status:** Not started. Content backlog exists but unpublished.

---

## Tier 2

### 5. Affiliate programme nurture sequence

**What it is:** A dedicated email sequence that takes someone who has been through the welcome sequence (or who is already on the list) and progressively makes the case for becoming a Tahi affiliate. This is not a hard sell. It is a series of emails that explains what the programme is, shows social proof from existing affiliates (when that exists), answers objections, and ends with a direct invitation to sign up.

**Why it matters:** Liam has flagged the affiliate programme as the highest ROI activity available. That is correct. A single affiliate who refers two clients per year is worth tens of thousands of NZD. The freelancer audience on the MailerLite list is exactly the right audience: they have clients who need websites, they have credibility with those clients, and they are incentivised by a 10% lifetime commission. The bottleneck is that almost none of them know the programme exists in enough detail to act on it.

**Progressive profiling opportunity:** Each whitepaper or resource download reveals more about a subscriber's work and clients. Over time, Nodeo (or a similar tool) can surface the right affiliate pitch at the right moment based on what someone has already downloaded and engaged with.

**What it needs:** Welcome sequence (item 1) is now built. Three to five emails written specifically for affiliate recruitment. Then ongoing case studies or proof points as affiliates start generating results.

**Automation potential:** High. Sequence is evergreen once written.

**Status:** Not started. Now unblocked (welcome sequence complete).

---

### 6. Whitepaper and lead magnet expansion

**What it is:** A library of downloadable resources beyond the Project Calculator. Each one targets a specific interest or problem in the freelancer or agency owner audience. Examples: a guide to Webflow Client First for beginners, a Figma-to-Webflow handoff checklist, a pricing guide for web projects, a Webflow integrations comparison.

**Why it matters:** Each download is a new list entry point, a new trust signal, and a new progressive profiling data point. Someone who downloads three different resources has revealed a lot about their interests and where they are in their career. That data feeds better segmentation, better timing for the affiliate pitch, and eventually better personalisation when Nodeo is live.

**What it needs:** Topics decided, content written (much of it already in Liam's head), a simple gated download mechanism on the Tahi site, and an automation in MailerLite that tags each download against the subscriber record.

**Automation potential:** High on the distribution side. Each lead magnet gets its own automation. Build once, runs forever.

**Status:** Not started. Ideas exist but none built.

---

### 7. Content system and publishing rhythm

**What it is:** A repeatable system that turns one piece of content into multiple outputs across channels. Two blog posts per week become two company LinkedIn posts, feed the monthly newsletter, and inspire personal LinkedIn posts for Liam. Code snippets become standalone posts and email content. The system means the content effort is not duplicated, just repurposed.

**Proposed cadence:**
- Blog: 2x per week (primary content engine, feeds everything else)
- Liam personal LinkedIn: 3x per week (mix of blog-derived posts and original short-form)
- Company LinkedIn: 2x per week (blog posts) plus 1 custom post every two weeks (showcase, code snippet, etc.)
- Code snippet: 1x every two to three weeks (new entry point and email content)
- Newsletter: 1x per month (curated from the month's best content plus personal reflection)

**Why it matters:** Consistency builds algorithmic reach on LinkedIn and search ranking on the blog simultaneously. The audience grows passively while the active work is being done.

**What it needs:** A content calendar, a simple brief template for blog posts, and the Zapier blog-to-LinkedIn flow confirmed as working. The hardest part is establishing the writing habit. AI can help with first drafts significantly.

**Automation potential:** Medium to high. The distribution layer (Zapier, scheduling tools) can be mostly automated. The writing cannot.

**Status:** Partial. Zapier blog-to-LinkedIn is in place. No consistent cadence running.

---

## Tier 3

### 8. Template library and launch sequences

**What it is:** Paid Webflow templates sold to the freelancer and agency owner audience. Two templates are at 95% completion. Each launch gets a three-email sequence: preview, launch, last chance.

**Why it matters:** Templates are a scalable product that requires no client relationship to sell. They serve the existing list directly. Revenue per launch at 1% conversion on 3,695 active subscribers and a $149 price point is approximately $5,500 per launch. With a warm list built by the welcome sequence and newsletter, conversion rates should exceed that.

**What it needs:** Templates finished and published. Launch sequence written (three emails). A simple landing page per template.

**Automation potential:** High. Launch sequences are evergreen and can run as limited-time automations.

**Status:** Two templates at 95%. Not yet launched.

---

### 9. Nodeo pre-launch and waitlist sequence

**What it is:** A dedicated segment of interested subscribers who are nurtured through the development of Nodeo. When the product is ready to launch, this segment receives a pre-launch sequence that converts waitlist to paying customers or early adopters.

**Why it matters:** Launching a product to a cold list is hard. Launching to a segment that has been following development, understands the problem it solves, and trusts the brand is significantly easier. Building this segment costs nothing except a few emails and a tag in MailerLite.

**What it needs:** A Nodeo User field already exists in MailerLite. A simple segment built from that field. A series of occasional product update emails that build anticipation without committing to a timeline.

**Status:** Nodeo User field exists. No sequence built. No segment created.

---

## Marketing Influences Worth Studying

The following people do this well and Tahi's strategy should draw from them, adapted for the Webflow and freelancer context.

**Alex Hormozi** (primary reference). Volume of value before the ask. The $100M Offers framing is directly applicable to how Tahi packages templates, the affiliate programme, and eventually courses. Core principle: make the free stuff so good that people cannot believe the paid stuff. The Project Calculator is already this. The code snippets and whitepapers are the next version of it.

**Justin Welsh** (LinkedIn personal brand). His system for building an audience on LinkedIn through consistent, short-form, personal content is the playbook for Liam's personal posts. The key insight: document, do not perform. Share what you are actually doing and thinking, not polished marketing content.

**Nathan Barry / ConvertKit** (email-first audience building). Nathan's writing on building an audience through email is directly applicable. Especially relevant: the concept of "teaching everything you know" as the primary list growth strategy. Every tutorial, code snippet, and guide Tahi publishes is this in practice.

**Katelyn Bourgoin** (buyer psychology in email). Her approach to understanding why people actually buy is useful for both the affiliate pitch and the template launch sequences. The framing: what is the job the buyer is hiring this for?

**Chris Do / The Futur** (creative business positioning). Directly relevant audience overlap. The way The Futur builds trust with designers and creatives before selling anything is the model for how Tahi should approach the freelancer audience.

**Dave Gerhardt / Exit Five** (B2B brand marketing). Useful for thinking about how to build Tahi's brand with potential clients, not just the freelancer audience. His principle that brand marketing is the most underrated B2B lever is worth taking seriously as Tahi grows.

---

## On the Re-Engagement Question

The concern about running a hard permission pass (click to confirm or get removed) is valid. The risk is real: most inactive subscribers will not click, the list drops by potentially 60 to 70%, and some of those people would have engaged with a future freebie or valuable piece of content.

The better approach for this list, informed by how Hormozi thinks about audience building: do not force the issue. Start sending good content consistently. Give the list multiple reasons to open and click over the next six months. Let the uninterested self-select out naturally by unsubscribing over time. After six months of regular sending (welcome sequence plus newsletter plus occasional launches), suppress anyone who has received at least eight emails and opened none of them. By then you have given them every reasonable chance. The people who remain unengaged at that point are genuinely not going to convert on anything.

This approach is gentler on list size, aligned with the value-first philosophy, and produces a cleaner result over time without the aggressive churn of a single permission pass.

---

*Status key: Not started / In progress / Done*
*Update this doc as priorities shift or tasks complete. Do not archive it.*

# 13: Nodeo
*Version 1.0, Last updated: April 2026*
*References: 11-tools-and-tech-stack.md, 14-marketing-strategy.md*
*Referenced by: 27-growth-priorities-2026.md*

---

## What Nodeo Is

Nodeo turns any Webflow form into a progressive two-step adaptive form. Same form on the page, but each visitor sees a curated subset of the questions based on what they have already answered on previous visits. Visit one collects an email and one or two priority fields. Visit two adds the next two. By visit three or four the full profile is built, without anyone ever facing a 22-field wall.

The product solves a specific problem the Webflow ecosystem has not solved: long lead-gen forms kill conversion, but short forms produce thin records that sales and marketing cannot work with. Progressive profiling fixes both ends. Nodeo is the implementation that makes it work natively inside Webflow with custom attributes, no code editing required by the customer.

---

## How It Works for the Customer

A customer signs up at getnodeo.com, picks a plan, configures their form steps in the Nodeo dashboard, then drops two custom attributes onto an existing Webflow form. The Nodeo script handles everything else: which fields to show on which visit, where to send the data, how to recognise repeat visitors, and how to surface the results back in the dashboard's reporting view.

The architecture mirrors how Webflow itself works. Customers do not write code. They configure attributes, click publish, watch the data come in. Anyone who has used Webflow for an afternoon can install Nodeo in five minutes.

---

## Target User

The first wave of Nodeo customers overlaps heavily with Tahi's own ICP and the Webflow community Tahi participates in. Specifically:

- Webflow-using SaaS marketing teams running lead-gen forms.
- Agencies who build Webflow sites for clients and want a recurring revenue add-on (resell Nodeo as part of every site they ship).
- Indie founders running their own Webflow site who would otherwise drop fields to chase conversion.
- Anyone running gated content on Webflow who wants a richer record of their downloaders without adding form friction.

The audience overlap with Tahi's own freelancer + agency target is intentional. Nodeo customers are likely Tahi prospects, and Tahi clients are pre-qualified Nodeo prospects.

---

## Pricing

Five tiers, self-serve via Stripe checkout. Limits are enforced server-side and per billing cycle.

| Plan | Forms | Sessions per cycle | Sites | Seats | $/month |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Free (maintain) | 1 | 750 | 1 | 1 | $0 |
| Pro (scale) | 5 | 5,000 | 1 | 1 | $19 |
| Business (tune) | 10 | 50,000 | 5 | 3 | $39 |
| Agency | unlimited | 150,000 | unlimited | 10 | $59 |
| Enterprise | unlimited | unlimited | unlimited | unlimited | custom |

Prices are not finalised yet. Treat the numbers above as the target shape. The agency plan exists specifically for the resell-to-clients motion.

---

## Marketing Role for Tahi

Nodeo serves Tahi in three ways beyond the SaaS revenue itself.

**Proof of capability.** A Webflow agency that has built and shipped its own SaaS product on top of Webflow is a different kind of agency. The fact that Nodeo exists makes Tahi's pitch ("we go deeper than the typical agency") concrete instead of aspirational.

**Top-of-funnel for the affiliate and template programmes.** Webflow folks who try Nodeo and like it are the right kind of people for Tahi's templates and the affiliate programme. The product itself becomes a discovery channel.

**Audience-building under one roof.** Nodeo and Tahi share the same ideal-customer profile and the same content audience. Marketing efforts on either side compound for the other.

---

## Status (as of April 2026)

The backend is roughly 80% production-ready. AWS Lambda + RDS PostgreSQL + CloudFront, all in eu-central-1. SAM-deployed. Single-tenant per workspace, multi-tenant per database, with admin scoping enforced at every handler.

The dashboard is real. Sign-up, sign-in, organisation switching, billing, plan limits, form builder, step builder, reporting, settings — all wired and shipping. Hosted on Webflow Cloud at getnodeo.com/app. Auth is Clerk-only as of the April 2026 migration; the previous Outseta and Memberstack paths are removed.

A super-admin surface for the founders ships alongside it: workspace browser, drill-down, view-as customer (audited), real-time signals, needs-attention queue, audit log, hard-delete with cascade.

The trajectory from here to a public launch is roughly three to five weeks of focused work, broken into three parallel streams: bug sweep and UX polish (one week), the two pre-launch features (Nodeo Structure popup and Webflow Designer App, three to six weeks dominated by Marketplace review timing), and the security and GDPR audit and policy docs (one week). Detail in the launch-readiness working note in the Nodeo repo.

---

## Launch Posture

The first launch is a private beta via invite link to a curated cohort, not an open signup. This buys time to fix the rough edges that always surface with real users without burning the wider audience. Pricing stays in test mode through beta. Stripe and Clerk both flip to live keys in a single coordinated cutover when the team is confident the product is ready.

After beta proves stable, the open public launch follows. The Webflow Designer App ships as a second wave once Marketplace approval lands.

---

## GDPR and Privacy Posture

Nodeo holds end-user data on behalf of customers. The posture is deliberately strong:

- Data resides in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt), encrypted at rest, TLS 1.2 minimum in transit.
- End-user emails are never stored in plaintext. They are HMAC-SHA256 hashed with a per-website + global salt before insertion into the EndUsers table.
- The Nodeo script that runs on the customer's site sets zero cookies, zero localStorage, zero sessionStorage. End users are recognised server-side via the email hash on next form submission. No cookie banner is required for Nodeo's part of the data flow.
- IP addresses are never stored. The full raw user-agent is never stored. Only parsed browser and OS class strings live in the PageViews table.
- The actual form responses (what users type into form fields) are submitted to Webflow's form endpoint, not Nodeo's. Nodeo tracks progress (started, completed, when), not content.

A formal GDPR audit was performed in April 2026. Three critical findings, four high, five medium. None require the product to come off the air. The remediation pack is roughly five working days plus a two-hour legal review on the policy documents. Detail in the audit findings note in the Nodeo repo.

---

## Roadmap to Launch

In rough priority order:

1. Bug sweep and UX polish across every customer-facing page. One week.
2. Nodeo Structure popup. A modal on every form that mimics the Webflow Designer Navigator panel exactly, with a side panel showing the precise custom attributes to add for that form. Self-contained, ships entirely on the Nodeo side. One to two weeks.
3. Webflow Designer App. A Marketplace-published Designer Extension that lives inside Webflow's Apps panel and writes Nodeo attributes onto the customer's selected element. Build is two weeks; Marketplace approval is one to four weeks of asynchronous review.
4. Security and GDPR remediation pack. The privacy notice, DPA template, sub-processor list, retention policy, internal DSAR runbook, internal breach response runbook. About a day and a half of writing plus legal review.
5. Stripe and Clerk live-key cutover. Single coordinated deploy.
6. Beta launch via invite link to a curated cohort.
7. Open public launch.

---

## Internal References

- Repo: github.com/GetNodeo/Nodeo (monorepo with backend lambdas + docs) and github.com/tahistudio/nodeo-dashboard (canonical dashboard, Webflow Cloud auto-deploys from main).
- Live dashboard: getnodeo.com/app and getnodeo.webflow.io/app.
- Working notes for launch: docs/launch-prep/launch-readiness.md, gdpr-audit-findings-2026-04-30.md, nodeo-structure-popup-scope.md, webflow-designer-app-scope.md, security-audit-scope.md, gdpr-audit-scope.md.
